name: Analytics
summary: Provides tools for sending Google Analytics events using the Measurement Protocol. See https://developers.google.com/analytics/devguides/collection/protocol/v1/devguide#event for details.

params.name: Name
params.doc: Name of Application. For example, "Ocelot". Do not include version numbers. Maps to Google Analytics parameter 'an'

constructors.name: Analytics(String, String, String, String, String)
constructors.signature: public Analytics(string TrackingID, string Name, string Platform, string InstallerId, string Version)

params.name: Name
params.doc: Name of Application. For example, "Ocelot". Do not include version numbers. Maps to Google Analytics parameter 'an'

params.name: Platform
params.doc: Platform application is running on. For example "Mac", "Windows". Again, don't include version numbers. Maps to Google Analytics parameter 'ai'

params.name: InstallerId
params.doc: App Installer Id. In Rhino, we use this to differentiate between different builds such as "WIP" and "Commercial". Maps to Google Analytics parameter 'aiid'

params.name: Version
params.doc: Application version string. Maps to Google Analytics parameter 'av'

methods.name: Send(NameValueCollection)
methods.summary: Advanced method for sending Google Analytics data. It is the caller's responsibility to make sure that all parameters passed will result in a valid Google Analytics hit. Failure to do so will result in Google Analytics ignoring your hit, and the caller will get no data. The Analytics class will populate data from the Application, the GoogleAnalyticsTrackingID, the User ID, and set the hit type "t" to "event". It also sets other information about the system.

params.name: data
params.doc: Name-Value pairs of data to send. Any valid Google Analytics Measurement Protocol parameter is allowed. No input validation is performed.

methods.name: Send(String)
methods.summary: Sends a Google Analytics event using the Measurement Protocol. See https://developers.google.com/analytics/devguides/collection/protocol/v1/devguide#event for details.

params.name: Category
params.doc: Event category. We use the feature or subsystem, such as "installer" or "app" or "document" or "loft". Maps to the Google Analytics parameter "ec".

methods.name: Send(String, String)
methods.signature: public void Send(string Category, string Action)
methods.summary: Sends a Google Analytics event using the Measurement Protocol. See https://developers.google.com/analytics/devguides/collection/protocol/v1/devguide#event for details.

params.name: Category
params.doc: Event category. We use the feature or subsystem, such as "installer" or "app" or "document" or "loft". Maps to the Google Analytics parameter "ec".

params.name: Action
params.doc: Event action. A verb: "open" or "start" or "option" Maps to the Google Analytics parameter "ea".

methods.name: Send(String, String, String)
methods.signature: public void Send(string Category, string Action, string Label)
methods.summary: Sends a Google Analytics event using the Measurement Protocol. See https://developers.google.com/analytics/devguides/collection/protocol/v1/devguide#event for details.

params.name: Category
params.doc: Event category. We use the feature or subsystem, such as "installer" or "app" or "document" or "loft". Maps to the Google Analytics parameter "ec".

params.name: Action
params.doc: Event action. A verb: "open" or "start" or "option" Maps to the Google Analytics parameter "ea".

params.name: Label
params.doc: Event label. Maps to the Google Analytics parameter "el".

methods.name: Send(String, String, String, UInt32)
methods.signature: public void Send(string Category, string Action, string Label, uint Value)
methods.summary: Sends a Google Analytics event using the Measurement Protocol. See https://developers.google.com/analytics/devguides/collection/protocol/v1/devguide#event for details. This method is asyncrhonous and the return code is completely ignored. Validate the data you send

params.name: Category
params.doc: Event category. We use the feature or subsystem, such as "installer" or "app" or "document" or "loft". Maps to the Google Analytics parameter "ec".

params.name: Action
params.doc: Event action. A verb: "open" or "start" or "option" Maps to the Google Analytics parameter "ea".

params.name: Label
params.doc: Event label. Maps to the Google Analytics parameter "el".

params.name: Value
params.doc: Event value. Maps to the Google Analytics parameter "ev".

properties.name: AppInstallerId
properties.summary: App Installer Id. In Rhino, we use this to differentiate between different builds such as "WIP" and "Commercial". Maps to Google Analytics parameter 'aiid'

properties.name: AppName
properties.summary: Name of Application. For example, "Ocelot". Do not include version numbers. Maps to Google Analytics parameter 'an'

properties.name: AppPlatform
properties.summary: Platform application is running on. For example "Mac", "Windows". Again, don't include version numbers. Maps to Google Analytics parameter 'ai'

properties.name: AppVersion
properties.summary: Application version string. Maps to Google Analytics parameter 'av'

properties.name: UsageStatisticsEnabled
properties.summary: Determine if user allows automatic data collection from Rhino

properties.name: UserId
properties.summary: Returns a GUID that allows events to be aggregated by user. There is no way to determine who the end user is based on this GUID, unless the user tells you their ID. On Windows, this uses the registry to store the ID. On Mac, the Hardware UUID is used as the ID.
